description:
Two cleanup items:
1. bd prime currently shows just --list output - remove this
2. The claude code hook in this repo should be removed; leave it configured globally instead
title: Remove just --list from bd prime; remove repo hook in favor of global

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration tests cover multiple representative old-store fixtures beyond the minimal v0.1.26 case
- [ ] Coverage includes tombstones, deleted deps, multiple embedded notes, closed/claimed beads, and legacy label variants
- [ ] Each fixture runs through detect plus migrate-to-1 and verifies strict-load success after rewrite
- [ ] Fixture provenance and intended scenario coverage are documented
description: The current migration compatibility gate proves the exact minimal v0.1.26 wire boundary we were most worried about, but it does not cover the full range of old-store variants users may have in the wild. In particular, we still lack representative fixtures for richer v0.1.x stores with multiple beads, tombstones, mixed workflow/claim states, multiple notes, deleted deps, and other legacy field combinations that may have accumulated before the hard cutover.
design: Build a small matrix of source-locked old-store fixtures derived from historical v0.1.x wire shapes, then run current migrate detect and migrate to 1 against each fixture in integration tests. Cover representative real-world combinations rather than every combinatorial possibility: active and deleted deps, tombstones, closed and claimed beads, multiple embedded notes, legacy labels arrays, and stores with local/remote divergence. Keep fixtures auditable with provenance notes and a reproducible generator path where practical.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 03adab51: added a checked-in legacy store corpus with provenance notes under crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus plus a loader helper and migration integration coverage for rich workflow state, tombstones, deleted deps, embedded notes, legacy label arrays, remote-only detect/migrate, related divergence, and unrelated-divergence force behavior. Fixture content was distilled from the real local repo mcp_dspy_eveals plus parser regression shapes for deleted legacy deps.

title: Expand migration coverage for real-world v0.1.x store variants

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IPC client tests restore the prior runtime-dir override after each mutation
- [ ] Neighboring tests no longer depend on execution order for socket-dir resolution
- [ ] beads-surface unit tests stay green under default parallelism
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs` test coverage mutates the process-global runtime-dir override and does not restore it. That makes neighboring unit tests order-dependent inside the same test binary.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs`
design: Add an RAII guard (or equivalent capture/restore helper) for the runtime-dir override in tests so each test restores the previous state on drop. Use a small local mutex only if the override API still needs explicit serialization.
title: Restore runtime-dir override after beads-surface IPC client tests
